prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the sky be blue, eh? Well, ye see, me ol' eyes ain't always this color. When the sun be shinin' bright an' the clouds be floatin' by, the sky gets painted in forget-me-not hues. 'Tis the way the light from that fiery celestial orb bounces about, scattered 'ere an' yon. The blue bits o' light get scattered the most, an' that's what ye gaze upon when ye look up at that vast expanse. 'Tis no magic trick, me hearty, just a workin' of the universe's grand design. So next time ye be wonderin' why the sky be blue, just remember 'tis all part of the grand adventure of livin' on this here Earth! Yarrr!
